_license: Original content for Open Dungeon Master. One plain-language line per SRD class feature that has no rules text elsewhere in the app, written for a player who has never played 5e. These state mechanics in our own wording; no publisher's descriptive text is reproduced. See docs/LICENSES.md.
_shape: features[name] = text. byClass[classId][name] = text overrides it where two classes give the same feature name different meanings. Resolution order lives in src/lib/help/index.ts; scripts/test-help-coverage.mjs fails if any grantable feature ends up with no text.

features.Additional Fighting Style: You pick a second Fighting Style, and both apply at once.
features.Additional Magical Secrets: You learn two more spells from any class's list. They count as bard spells for you.
features.Arcane Tradition: The marker for choosing your wizard school. The school you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Archdruid: You can Wild Shape as often as you like, and your druid spells no longer need spoken or gestured components.
features.Artificer Specialist: The marker for choosing your artificer specialty. The specialty you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Aura Improvements: Your paladin auras reach 30 feet instead of 10, so they cover most of the party in a fight.
features.Aura of Courage: You and allies within range cannot be frightened while you are conscious.
features.Aura of Devotion: You and allies within range cannot be charmed while you are conscious.
features.Bard College: The marker for choosing your bard college. The college you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Beast Spells: While you are Wild Shaped into a beast, you can still cast your druid spells.
features.Blessed Healer: When you heal someone else with a spell, you also heal yourself a little.
features.Blindsense: You know the location of any hidden or invisible creature within 10 feet, as long as you can hear.
features.Bonus Cantrip: You learn one extra cantrip from your class's spell list.
features.Bonus Proficiencies (Lore): You gain proficiency in three skills of your choice.
features.Bonus Proficiencies (Tempest): You can use martial weapons and wear heavy armor.
features.Bonus Proficiency (heavy armor): You can wear heavy armor without penalty.
features.Circle Spells: Extra spells tied to your circle. They are always prepared and do not count against the number you can prepare.
features.Cleansing Touch: You can end one spell affecting yourself or a creature you touch, a few times per long rest.
features.Countercharm: As an action, you perform for a moment and give yourself and nearby allies advantage on saves against being frightened or charmed.
features.Cunning Action: Each turn you can Dash, Disengage or Hide as a bonus action, on top of your normal action.
features.Cutting Words: As a reaction, spend a Bardic Inspiration die to subtract it from an enemy's attack roll, ability check or damage roll.
features.Dark One's Blessing: When you drop an enemy to 0 hit points, you gain temporary hit points.
features.Dark One's Own Luck: Once per short or long rest, add a d10 to an ability check or saving throw after rolling.
features.Defensive Tactics: A defensive option from your ranger archetype: shrug off multiattacks, escape opportunity attacks, or resist being frightened by beasts.
features.Deflect Missiles: As a reaction, reduce the damage of a ranged weapon attack; if you reduce it to 0 you can throw the missile back.
features.Destroy Undead (CR 1): Turning now destroys undead up to challenge rating 1 outright.
features.Destroy Undead (CR 1/2): Weak undead you turn with Channel Divinity are destroyed outright instead of merely fleeing.
features.Destroy Undead (CR 2): Turning now destroys undead up to challenge rating 2 outright.
features.Destroy Undead (CR 3): Turning now destroys undead up to challenge rating 3 outright.
features.Destroy Undead (CR 4): Turning now destroys undead up to challenge rating 4 outright.
features.Diamond Soul: You are proficient in every saving throw, and can spend ki to reroll one you fail.
features.Disciple of Life: Your healing spells restore extra hit points, scaling with the spell's level.
features.Divine Domain: The marker for choosing your cleric domain. The domain you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Divine Intervention: Once per long rest you can call on your god for direct aid. Success is not guaranteed at first, and the DM decides what arrives.
features.Divine Intervention Improvement: Your call for divine aid now always works.
features.Divine Strike: Once per turn, your weapon hits deal an extra damage die of your domain's type. The die grows at higher levels.
features.Draconic Presence: You can spend sorcery points to awe or terrify creatures around you, charming or frightening those who fail a save.
features.Draconic Resilience: Your hit point maximum rises with your level, and with no armor your AC is 13 plus your Dexterity modifier.
features.Dragon Ancestor: You choose a kind of dragon. It sets the damage type your bloodline favours, and you can speak Draconic.
features.Dragon Wings: As a bonus action you sprout wings and gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed.
features.Druid Circle: The marker for choosing your druid circle. The circle you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Druidic: You know the secret language of druids and can leave hidden messages only other druids notice.
features.Eldritch Invocations: Permanent magical perks you choose and can swap when you level: at-will spells, better Eldritch Blast, darkvision and more.
features.Eldritch Master: Once per long rest you can spend a minute pleading with your patron to regain all your Pact Magic spell slots.
features.Elemental Affinity: Add your Charisma modifier to the damage of spells matching your dragon ancestor, and spend a sorcery point for resistance to that damage.
features.Elusive: No attack roll against you has advantage while you are conscious.
features.Empowered Evocation: Add your Intelligence modifier to the damage of your evocation spells.
features.Empty Body: Spend ki to turn invisible for a minute, or spend more to cast astral projection without components.
features.Evocation Savant: Copying evocation spells into your spellbook costs half the usual time and gold.
features.Expertise: You double your proficiency bonus on two skills or tools of your choice, so you rarely fail at them.
features.Extra Attack: You attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action.
features.Fast Hands: Use your Cunning Action bonus action to make a Sleight of Hand check, use thieves' tools, or use an object.
features.Favored Enemy: You have advantage on tracking a kind of creature and remembering facts about it, and you learn a related language.
features.Favored Enemy Improvement: You choose an additional favored enemy and another language.
features.Feral Instinct: You have advantage on initiative, and being surprised no longer stops you acting if you enter a rage first.
features.Feral Senses: You can fight invisible creatures without disadvantage, and you sense unseen creatures within 30 feet.
features.Fiendish Resilience: After each rest, choose a damage type; you resist it until you choose again.
features.Fighting Style: A combat specialty you choose once, such as Archery for ranged accuracy or Defense for extra armor class. It applies automatically.
features.Flash of Genius: As a reaction, add your Intelligence modifier to an ability check or saving throw made by you or an ally within 30 feet.
features.Foe Slayer: Once per turn, add your Wisdom modifier to an attack roll or damage roll against your favored enemy.
features.Font of Inspiration: Your Bardic Inspiration comes back on a short rest, not just a long one.
features.Frenzy: While raging you can go into a frenzy, making a bonus-action melee attack each turn at the cost of a level of exhaustion afterwards.
features.Hide in Plain Sight: Spend a minute camouflaging yourself against a solid surface for a large bonus to hiding, until you move.
features.Holy Nimbus: Once per long rest, blaze with light for a minute: enemies near you take radiant damage and you resist their spells.
features.Hunter's Prey: A damage option from your ranger archetype: extra damage on a wounded target, a second attack against a nearby enemy, or a bonus against large foes.
features.Hurl Through Hell: Once per long rest, a creature you hit is dragged briefly through the lower planes and takes heavy psychic damage.
features.Improved Divine Smite: Every melee weapon hit you land deals an extra 1d8 radiant damage, with no spell slot needed.
features.Indomitable (1 use): Once per long rest you can reroll a saving throw you failed.
features.Indomitable (2 uses): You can reroll a failed saving throw twice per long rest.
features.Indomitable (3 uses): You can reroll a failed saving throw three times per long rest.
features.Indomitable Might: If a Strength check comes up lower than your Strength score, use your Strength score instead.
features.Infuse Item: You know a list of infusions and can imbue mundane gear with them after a long rest, turning ordinary items into magic ones for your party.
features.Intimidating Presence: As an action, frighten one creature within 30 feet that fails a Wisdom save.
features.Jack of All Trades: Add half your proficiency bonus to any ability check that does not already use it, so you are decent at everything.
features.Land's Stride: Difficult terrain from plants costs you no extra movement, and magical plants cannot slow or hurt you without a save.
features.Magic Item Adept: You can attune to four magic items at once, and you craft common and uncommon items in a quarter of the usual time and cost.
features.Magic Item Master: You can attune to six magic items at once.
features.Magic Item Savant: You can attune to five magic items at once, and you ignore all class, race, spell and level requirements on using them.
features.Magical Secrets: You learn two spells from any class's list. They count as bard spells for you.
features.Magical Tinkering: You imbue Tiny objects with a small magical property: light, a recorded message, a smell, or a picture. You can have as many as your Intelligence modifier.
features.Martial Archetype: The marker for choosing your fighter archetype. The archetype you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Metamagic: Ways to bend a spell as you cast it by spending sorcery points: cast it faster, at longer range, at two targets, or with a reroll on the damage.
features.Metamagic Option: You learn one more Metamagic option.
features.Monastic Tradition: The marker for choosing your monk tradition. The tradition you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Multiattack: An archetype option: either a volley of ranged attacks against everyone in an area, or a whirlwind of melee attacks against everyone next to you.
features.Mystic Arcanum (6th level): One 6th-level spell your patron grants. You can cast it once per long rest without using a spell slot.
features.Mystic Arcanum (7th level): One 7th-level spell you can cast once per long rest without using a spell slot.
features.Mystic Arcanum (8th level): One 8th-level spell you can cast once per long rest without using a spell slot.
features.Mystic Arcanum (9th level): One 9th-level spell you can cast once per long rest without using a spell slot.
features.Natural Explorer: In your favored terrain you travel faster, cannot get lost, stay alert while travelling, and find more food.
features.Natural Explorer Improvement: You choose an additional favored terrain.
features.Nature's Sanctuary: Beasts and plants must pass a save to attack you at all.
features.Nature's Ward: You cannot be charmed or frightened by elementals or fey, and you are immune to poison and disease.
features.Open Hand Technique: When you hit with Flurry of Blows you can also knock the target prone, push it 15 feet, or stop it taking reactions.
features.Otherworldly Patron: The marker for choosing the being you made your pact with. The patron you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Overchannel: Deal maximum damage with a damaging spell of 5th level or lower. Using it more than once between long rests hurts you badly.
features.Pact Boon: A gift from your patron: a summonable weapon, a book of extra cantrips, or a familiar that can scout for you.
features.Pact Magic: Warlock spellcasting. You have few slots but they are always cast at their highest level and come back on a short rest.
features.Peerless Skill: Spend a Bardic Inspiration die on your own ability check.
features.Perfect Self: If you roll initiative with no ki left, you regain 4 ki points.
features.Potent Cantrip: Creatures that succeed on a saving throw against your cantrip still take half damage.
features.Primal Champion: Your Strength and Constitution each rise by 4, past the usual maximum of 20.
features.Primal Path: The marker for choosing your barbarian path. The path you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Primeval Awareness: Spend a spell slot to sense whether certain creature types are nearby, though not where or how many.
features.Purity of Body: You are immune to disease and poison.
features.Purity of Spirit: You are always under the effect of protection from evil and good, so fiends and undead struggle to touch or charm you.
features.Quivering Palm: Spend ki to set lethal vibrations in a creature. Later you can end them as an action, forcing a Constitution save or death.
features.Ranger Archetype: The marker for choosing your ranger archetype. The archetype you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Reckless Attack: Attack with abandon on your turn: you get advantage on your melee Strength attacks, but everyone has advantage against you until your next turn.
features.Remarkable Athlete: Add half your proficiency bonus to Strength, Dexterity and Constitution checks that do not already use it, and your running long jump is longer.
features.Retaliation: When a creature within 5 feet damages you, use your reaction to attack it back.
features.Roguish Archetype: The marker for choosing your rogue archetype. The archetype you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Sacred Oath: The marker for choosing your paladin oath. The oath you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Sculpt Spells: Choose a few creatures to automatically pass their saves against your evocation spells and take no damage, so fireballs stop hitting your friends.
features.Second-Story Work: Climbing costs you no extra movement, and your running jump is longer than normal.
features.Signature Spells: Two 3rd-level spells are always prepared and can each be cast once per short rest without a slot.
features.Slippery Mind: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws, which are the ones that resist mind control.
features.Slow Fall: As a reaction, reduce falling damage by five times your monk level.
features.Sorcerous Origin: The marker for choosing the source of your magic. The origin you pick supplies the actual abilities.
features.Sorcerous Restoration: You regain 4 sorcery points on a short rest.
features.Soul of Artifice: You gain +1 to every saving throw per attuned magic item, and you can end one attunement as a reaction to drop to 1 hit point instead of 0.
features.Spell Mastery: Choose a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell you can cast at will, without spending a slot.
features.Spell-Storing Item: After a long rest you store a 1st or 2nd-level artificer spell in an item, and whoever holds it can cast it a number of times based on your Intelligence.
features.Spellcasting: Your ability to cast spells: which list you learn from, how many spells you can have ready, and the slots you spend to cast them.
features.Stillness of Mind: As an action, end one effect charming or frightening you.
features.Stroke of Luck: Once per short or long rest, turn a missed attack into a hit or a failed ability check into a 20.
features.Stunning Strike: Spend 1 ki on a melee hit to stun the target until the end of your next turn unless it passes a Constitution save.
features.Superior Hunter's Defense: A defensive option from your ranger archetype: dodge one attack as a reaction, resist one damage type, or retaliate when an ally is hit.
features.Superior Inspiration: If you roll initiative with no Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use.
features.Supreme Healing: Your healing spells restore the maximum possible amount instead of rolling.
features.Supreme Sneak: You have advantage on Stealth checks if you move no more than half your speed on your turn.
features.Survivor: At the start of each of your turns you regain hit points if you are below half your maximum and not at 0.
features.The Right Tool for the Job: With tinker's tools and an hour's work you conjure any set of artisan's tools you need, once per rest.
features.Thief's Reflexes: You take two turns in the first round of combat, the second at initiative minus 10.
features.Thieves' Cant: A secret mix of slang and signs that lets you hide messages in ordinary conversation. Only other rogues understand it.
features.Tongue of the Sun and Moon: You understand every spoken language, and anyone who can hear you understands what you say.
features.Tool Expertise: You double your proficiency bonus on any ability check using tools you are proficient with.
features.Tranquility: Between rests you are under a sanctuary effect: creatures must pass a save to attack you at all.
features.Unarmored Defense: You get a good armor class without wearing armor. The server works it out from your ability scores.
features.Uncanny Dodge: As a reaction, halve the damage of one attack you can see coming.
features.Use Magic Device: You can use any magic item, ignoring the class, race or level requirements it normally carries.
features.Wholeness of Body: Once per long rest, an action heals you for three times your monk level.
features.Wild Shape Improvement: Your beast forms get better: a higher challenge rating, and at 8th level no restriction on swimming or flying.

monk.Timeless Body: You stop ageing outwardly and no longer need food or water.

druid.Timeless Body: You age at a tenth of the normal rate.
